messages.subject: 🗓️ March is here. Let’s plan it properly.
messages.body:
March has started.
A new month is always a good moment to pause and ask yourself:
 Where am I going? And am I moving in the right direction?
Planning your IELTS preparation for the month can completely change your results. Not just studying randomly. Not just "doing something." But following a clear, structured path. And if you're not sure what to focus on, how to organise your preparation, or how to get results faster -- you probably don't need more materials. You need guidance.
You need someone who sees the full picture, understands your level, and can show you the shortest path to your target score. 
That's exactly what my course is about. It's self-paced, but you're not alone. You get structured modules, personalised tasks, and feedback from me. I personally supervise your progress and adjust the strategy when needed.
If March is the month you finally decide to do this properly, this is your sign.
Join the course and let's build a clear plan for your score.
P.S. The course price will increase soon, so if you've been thinking about it, now is a good time.

messages.subject: Leading the Path to 6G in the AI Era
messages.body:
Qualcomm Vision

By Qualcomm


By Qualcomm

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/leading-path-6g-ai-era-qualcomm-bwsie?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BrCpr2FXmRw%2B1P7Tcd1PqCA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFXAeHu6LrQEQ&midSig=0jJtsSkggXMs81&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-miws1m~mm8wqjln~l3-null-null&eid=miws1m-mm8wqjln-l3
  
As AI becomes woven into every experience, the role of wireless is evolving. Future systems must be built to support intelligence as a native capability, operating seamlessly across devices, network infrastructure, and cloud platforms. This is the shift driving the design of 6G, and it’s where Qualcomm is focused.   At MWC Barcelona (March 2-5), we’re announcing a coordinated industry effort aimed at aligning the ecosystem early around the technical architecture foundations needed to bring 6G to market. Working alongside a group of leading partners, the initiative pledges a unified approach to how this technology is developed as a cohesive, AI-native platform.   By approaching 6G holistically, Qualcomm is laying the foundation for new experiences, new services, and new opportunities across consumer, enterprise, and industrial domains.  Throughout the week, Qualcomm Vision will bring special coverage from MWC, diving deep into the new era of 6G connectivity and other announcements that span our broader technology portfolio, offering a closer look at how Qualcomm is building the future of wireless in the AI era.  This week at MWC:   6G coalition: Qualcomm announced a strategic coalition with over 50 industry partners to accelerate 6G deployment, establishing a milestone-driven roadmap focused on early system validation, pre-commercial demonstrations, and initial global rollouts starting by 2029 onwards. The collaboration also centers on coordinated progress across devices, networks, and cloud infrastructure, with the objective of supporting the development of interoperable, scalable 6G systems.           > See how Qualcomm and our partners are advancing the path to 6G.   Collaboration with T-Mobile: Also announced as part of the broader 6G industry initiative is an agreement with T-Mobile, America’s leading 5G operator, that will advance the three core pillars of 6G: advanced connectivity, wide-area sensing, and energy efficient high-performance compute, in an effort to turn the vision of 6G into reality.     > Get the full details of this collaboration between Qualcomm and T-Mobile.   Moving from concept to proof with Ericsson: Another key milestone on the road to 6G is taking shape through collaboration with Ericsson, achieving technical alignment on fundamental 6G radio innovations. At MWC, this work will be showcased through live demonstrations that validate these advances in prototype environments, highlighting progress across new spectrum, future 6G specifications, and core performance foundations.  > More on this agreement here.   Snapdragon Wear Elite: Snapdragon is introducing Snapdragon Wear Elite, our newest platform designed to power always‑on, on‑device intelligence for the next generation of wearable devices. Built to support real‑time AI apps and experiences, this platform combines dedicated AI processing, advanced sensing, and ultra‑efficient performance to enable wearables that understand context, respond naturally, and work seamlessly with the rapidly growing ecosystem of user devices. With a broad, multi‑mode connectivity architecture and significant gains in performance and power efficiency, Snapdragon Wear Elite is designed to unlock new innovative form factors beyond traditional wearables, while fueling more intuitive and sophisticated personal AI experiences that move with you throughout your whole day, and beyond.   > Discover more about Snapdragon Wear Elite. Reimagining connectivity for the AI era with Wi-Fi 8: As AI reshapes how data moves across devices and networks, Qualcomm is redefining what networks and devices can do to meet the moment. At MWC, we’re unveiling a new, industry-leading Wi‑Fi 8 generation of products spanning mobile devices and networking infrastructure and designed to support AI‑driven experiences with higher performance, greater reliability, and extended range. Featuring the Qualcomm FastConnect 8800 Mobile Connectivity System and five new Qualcomm Dragonwing Networking Platforms spanning AI‑native routers, gateways, and access points, these new products establish a stronger, more intelligent foundation for next‑generation products and services.    > Explore more of our Wi‑Fi 8 portfolio announcement.  Qualcomm X105 5G Modem-RF: The new Qualcomm X105 Modem-RF marks a major step forward in 5G Advanced and a critical enabler for future 6G development. Built on a new architecture, it delivers significant gains in speed, power efficiency, and intelligence, with AI‑driven traffic optimization embedded directly into the modem. Industry‑first innovations (including advanced GNSS capabilities, integrated satellite connectivity, and a highly efficient RF design) expand performance across mobile experiences while supporting new use cases that demand higher throughput, greater uplink capacity, and precise location awareness. Commercial availability is expected in the second half of this year.   > Dive deeper into how Qualcomm X105 is advancing wireless connectivity. Accelerating AI‑native RAN for Telcos: At MWC, Qualcomm is introducing an Agentic RAN Management Service for the Dragonwing RAN Automation Suite, enabling a path to 6G-grade autonomous networks while delivering advanced capabilities for network performance and operating cost reduction today. In parallel, Qualcomm is announcing new RAN AI features available on its commercial Open RAN platforms. These AI‑driven capabilities for radio units and telco servers allow operators to apply AI value across Open RAN deployments to improve uplink and downlink performance and improve deployment cost efficiency ahead of the 6G-era.  > Read more here.  Executive Corner On Tuesday, March 3, Qualcomm President and CEO Cristiano Amon will take the MWC Barcelona keynote stage with Architecting 6G for the AI Era. As the industry begins to shape what comes after 5G, the path to 6G is being defined by a clear shift: AI everywhere. The architectural decisions made now will influence how intelligence moves across devices, networks, and data centers—and ultimately how people, businesses, and industries connect and interact. Amon will frame why this moment matters, and how long‑term architectural thinking will help guide the next generation of wireless in an AI‑native world. > Watch live on March 3 at 9:30 AM CET/12:30 AM PT. Thanks for reading Qualcomm Vision. We’ll be delivering daily coverage from MWC 2026 all week long, including recaps, product highlights, and key moments from Barcelona. Check back each day to stay up to date on everything Qualcomm is unveiling at the show.
Keep reading on LinkedIn

messages.subject: The Real Cost of Building a Generative AI App for the Enterprise
messages.body:
Appinventiv Weekly

By Appinventiv


By Appinventiv

    
    
    Read this article on LinkedIn to join the conversation
Read on LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/real-cost-building-generative-ai-app-enterprise-appinventiv-lt09c?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BaqWYtZ6%2BRH%2Bvo5U9CUETsg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFXAeHu6LrQEQ&midSig=1dRfsURR39Mc81&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-miws1m~mm8svyk0~a2-null-null&eid=miws1m-mm8svyk0-a2
  
The cost to build a generative AI app has become a serious line item in enterprise planning. Not because AI is trendy, but because leadership teams are now being asked to fund systems that actually run inside the business. This is no longer about demos or pilots. Enterprises are deploying generative AI across internal knowledge systems, customer workflows, operations, and decision support. Once AI touches core processes, cost discussions change fast. In practice, the cost to develop a generative AI app typically falls in the range of $40,000 to $400,000+. That spread exists for a reason. These systems are shaped more by business context than by the model itself. Why do Costs Vary so Widely All generative AI projects are not the same. On one end, there are focused tools that solve one workflow. On the other end, there are enterprise platforms that operate across teams and departments: Single-purpose internal tools A small AI tool built for a single internal task usually sits at the lower end of the cost range. These projects are tight in scope and solve one clear problem. In most cases, budgets range from $40,000 to $150,000+. Multi-team or multi-workflow systems Once the system needs to serve multiple teams or pull from several data sources, costs rise quickly. Mid-level enterprise builds often fall in the $150,000 to $250,000+ range because they demand stronger integrations and better controls. Enterprise-wide AI platforms At the high end are enterprise-wide platforms. These systems run across departments, handle sensitive data, and operate under strict security and compliance rules. Budgets here typically reach $250,000 to $400,000 or more. None of this is driven by ambition alone. It is driven by responsibility. What Enterprises Actually Pay For Most of the cost has little to do with “AI magic.” It comes from the work needed to make AI safe, reliable, and useful inside an organization. Data preparation is often the first surprise. Enterprise data lives across tools, formats, and teams. Making it usable takes effort, then comes architecture. Secure access, retrieval pipelines, audit trails, and performance controls do not build themselves. Integration work also adds weight. AI that cannot interact with your CRM, ERP, or internal platforms rarely survives past the first demo. And once security, identity management, and compliance enter the picture, shortcuts disappear. Development is Only Part of the Spend One common mistake is treating AI as a one-time build. In reality, costs continue after launch. Model usage, infrastructure scaling, monitoring, and regular improvements all matter. For enterprise teams, this is expected. AI behaves more like infrastructure than software features. When planned well, it delivers compounding value. When rushed, it becomes an ongoing cost with unclear returns. A Practical Way to Think About Investment Strong AI initiatives start narrow. They prove value early, and then they expand with control. Leaders who approach generative AI this way tend to stay within budget and avoid unpleasant surprises. Those who skip planning often learn the hard way. Generative AI is now part of enterprise reality. The question is not whether to build, but whether the system is designed to last.
Keep reading on LinkedIn
